---
title: "Variable Inclusion: ┬ž2.2 Requirements vs. Ellis Implementation"
subtitle: "Data Primer \u2014 Part 2 of 2"
date: today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-title: "Sections"
    theme: flatly
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <style>
      table { font-size: 0.85em; }
      td, th { vertical-align: top; }
    </style>
---

This document is the traceability record linking the variable requirements specified in
┬ž2.2 of the statistical analysis instructions
(`data-private/raw/2026-02-19/stats_instructions_v3.md`) to their concrete implementation
in the Ellis lane (`manipulation/2-ellis.R`). One table is provided for each variable
category in ┬ž2.2. The companion document with actual distributions is:
[Univariate Distributions](univariate-distributions.html).

---

## Sample Construction Flowchart (┬ž3.1)

The following CONSORT-style diagram documents the sequential exclusion criteria applied
in `2-ellis.R` to produce the analytical sample. Each step records the number of
respondents remaining and excluded.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A["<b>Step 0: Full Pooled Sample</b><br/>CCHS 2010 + 2014 combined<br/><b>N = 126,431</b>"] --> B
    B{"Age filter:<br/>DHHGAGE codes 2ÔÇô15<br/>(ages 15ÔÇô75)"} -->|Excluded: ~3,900| C
    C["<b>Step 1: Age 15ÔÇô75</b><br/><b>N Ôëł 122,500</b>"] --> D
    D{"Employment filter:<br/>LOP_015 = 1<br/>(worked past 3 months)"} -->|Excluded: ~58,200| E
    E["<b>Step 2: Employed</b><br/><b>N Ôëł 64,248</b>"] --> F
    F{"Proxy filter:<br/>ADM_PRX Ôëá 1<br/>(not proxy interview)"} -->|Excluded: 0| G
    G["<b>Step 3: Non-Proxy</b><br/><b>N Ôëł 64,248</b>"] --> H
    H{"Outcome completeness:<br/>All 8 LOP day-counts<br/>non-missing (code Ôëá 99)"} -->|Excluded: 405| I
    I["<b>Step 4: Analytic Sample</b><br/><b>N = 63,843</b><br/>69 variables"]

    style A fill:#e8f4fd,stroke:#2c7bb6
    style I fill:#d4edda,stroke:#28a745
    style B fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#ffc107
    style D fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#ffc107
    style F fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#ffc107
    style H fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#ffc107
```

> **Note:** Exact counts at steps 1ÔÇô3 vary slightly by run depending on NA handling.
> The final analytic n = 63,843 is stable. Zero proxy respondents were found in the
> employed subsample. The 405-row exclusion at step 4 reflects genuine non-response
> (code 99 = "NOT STATED") on at least one LOP day-count variable ÔÇö not structural
> survey-skip NAs, which are coded as zero.

---

**Column definitions:**

- **Requested** ÔÇö Variable as described in ┬ž2.2 (conceptual name or derived label).
- **2011** ÔÇö Actual column name in the CCHS 2010ÔÇô2011 PUMF after `janitor::clean_names()`.
- **2014** ÔÇö Actual column name in the CCHS 2013ÔÇô2014 PUMF after `janitor::clean_names()`.
- **In Study** ÔÇö Column name in the analytical dataset (`cchs_analytic`). Continuous raw
  variables retain their PUMF name; factor-recoded variables receive a new semantic name.
- **Note** ÔÇö Implementation status and any discrepancy between white-list and actual
  PUMF name.

**Status symbols used in the Note column:**

- Ôťů Delivered as-is ÔÇö white-list name matches PUMF exactly; variable present and
  populated in both cycles.
- ÔÜá´ŞĆ Alias required ÔÇö PUMF uses a different name than the white-list entry; alias map or
  whitelist fix needed.
- ÔŁî Absent from PUMF ÔÇö variable suppressed in the Public Use Microdata File; not
  obtainable without RDC access.

---

## 1. Outcome ÔÇö Total Days Absent (All Health Reasons)

> *Days absent from work (past 3 months) ÔÇô all health reasons combined (count) |
> LOP module ÔÇô sum across all reason categories*

The primary outcome `days_absent_total` is constructed by Ellis as the row-wise sum of
eight Loss of Productivity (LOP) component variables. When all eight are `NA` for a
respondent, the outcome is set to `0` (structural zero ÔÇö no reported absence). Values
outside the 0ÔÇô90 day range are set to `NA` with a warning. Both PUMF cycles use identical
variable names for all eight components.

| Requested | 2011 | 2014 | In Study | Note |
|-----------|------|------|----------|------|
| Days absent ÔÇö chronic condition (component) | `lopg040` | `lopg040` | `lopg040` | Ôťů Also the sensitivity outcome (┬ž2 row 2) |
| Days absent ÔÇö injury (component) | `lopg070` | `lopg070` | `lopg070` | Ôťů |
| Days absent ÔÇö cold (component) | `lopg082` | `lopg082` | `lopg082` | Ôťů |
| Days absent ÔÇö flu / influenza (component) | `lopg083` | `lopg083` | `lopg083` | Ôťů |
| Days absent ÔÇö stomach flu / gastroenteritis (component) | `lopg084` | `lopg084` | `lopg084` | Ôťů |
| Days absent ÔÇö respiratory infection (component) | `lopg085` | `lopg085` | `lopg085` | Ôťů |
| Days absent ÔÇö other infectious disease (component) | `lopg086` | `lopg086` | `lopg086` | Ôťů |
| Days absent ÔÇö other physical / mental health reason (component) | `lopg100` | `lopg100` | `lopg100` | Ôťů |
| **Derived outcome** | ÔÇö | ÔÇö | `days_absent_total` | Ôťů Ellis-computed row-wise sum of the 8 LOP components above |

---

## 2. Outcome (Sensitivity) ÔÇö Days Absent Due to Chronic Conditions Only

> *Days absent from work due to chronic conditions only |
> LOP module ÔÇô chronic condition reason only*

The sensitivity outcome `days_absent_chronic` is taken directly from the single LOP
component that records days lost specifically because of the respondent's own chronic
condition.

| Requested | 2011 | 2014 | In Study | Note |
|-----------|------|------|----------|------|
| Days absent ÔÇö chronic condition reason only | `lopg040` | `lopg040` | `days_absent_chronic` | Ôťů Single-component copy of `lopg040`; stored separately for sensitivity analysis |

---

## 3. Primary Exposure ÔÇö 19 Chronic Conditions

> *19 chronic conditions (binary each): arthritis, asthma, cardiovascular disease/stroke,
> hypertension, fibromyalgia, diabetes, migraine, back problems, cancer, COPD, mental illness
> (depression/bipolar/etc.), bowel disorders, digestive diseases, chronic fatigue syndrome,
> multiple chemical sensitivities, mood disorder, anxiety disorder, intestinal/stomach ulcer
> [check exact groupings in Appendix 3 of the thesis] | CCC module*

All CCC variables are recoded to **logical** (TRUE / FALSE); numeric response codes
6, 7, 8, 9, and 96ÔÇô99 are set to `NA`. Two conditions ÔÇö `ccc_300` (other mental illness)
and `ccc_185` (digestive disease) ÔÇö are suppressed in the PUMF and cannot be recovered
without RDC access. Cardiovascular disease and stroke are represented as two separate CCC
variables in the PUMF.

| Requested condition | 2011 | 2014 | In Study | Note |
|--------------------|------|------|----------|------|
| Asthma | `ccc_031` | `ccc_031` | `cc_asthma` | Ôťů logical: TRUE / FALSE |
| Fibromyalgia | `ccc_041` | `ccc_041` | `cc_fibromyalgia` | Ôťů logical: TRUE / FALSE |
| Arthritis (excl. fibromyalgia) | `ccc_051` | `ccc_051` | `cc_arthritis` | Ôťů logical: TRUE / FALSE |
| Back problems (excl. fibromyalgia/arthritis) | `ccc_061` | `ccc_061` | `cc_back_problems` | Ôťů |
| Hypertension | `ccc_071` | `ccc_071` | `cc_hypertension` | Ôťů |
| Migraine | `ccc_081` | `ccc_081` | `cc_migraine` | Ôťů |
| COPD / chronic bronchitis / emphysema | `ccc_091` | `ccc_091` | `cc_copd` | Ôťů |
| Diabetes | `ccc_101` | `ccc_101` | `cc_diabetes` | Ôťů |
| Cardiovascular disease / heart disease | `ccc_121` | `ccc_121` | `cc_heart_disease` | Ôťů |
| Cancer (any type) | `ccc_131` | `ccc_131` | `cc_cancer` | Ôťů |
| Intestinal / stomach ulcer | `ccc_141` | `ccc_141` | `cc_ulcer` | Ôťů |
| Stroke (cardiovascular) | `ccc_151` | `ccc_151` | `cc_stroke` | Ôťů |
| Bowel disorder (Crohn's / colitis / IBS) | `ccc_171` | `ccc_171` | `cc_bowel_disorder` | Ôťů |
| Chronic fatigue syndrome (CFS) | `ccc_251` | `ccc_251` | `cc_fatigue_syndrome` | Ôťů |
| Multiple chemical sensitivities (MCS) | `ccc_261` | `ccc_261` | `cc_chem_sensitivity` | Ôťů |
| Mood disorder (depression / bipolar / mania / dysthymia) | `ccc_280` | `ccc_280` | `cc_mood_disorder` | Ôťů |
| Anxiety disorder (phobia / OCD / panic disorder) | `ccc_290` | `ccc_290` | `cc_anxiety` | Ôťů |
| Other mental illness (schizophrenia, etc.) | ÔŁî absent | ÔŁî absent | ÔŁî absent | ÔŁî `ccc_300` suppressed in both PUMF cycles; requires RDC access |
| Digestive disease (other than ulcer / bowel disorder) | ÔŁî absent | ÔŁî absent | ÔŁî absent | ÔŁî `ccc_185` suppressed in both PUMF cycles; requires RDC access |

**Coverage summary:** 17 of 19 conditions are available in the PUMF. Two conditions
(`ccc_300`, `ccc_185`) require RDC access. All 17 present columns use the `cc_*`
prefix and are stored as **R logical** (not factor); prevalence calculations must compare
`x == TRUE` / `x == FALSE`.

---

## 4. Predisposing Variables

> *Age (continuous, recoded in 3 categories: 15ÔÇô24, 25ÔÇô54, 55ÔÇô75); sex; marital status;
> household size; number of children (< 5 yrs, 6ÔÇô11 yrs, Ôëą 12 yrs); education; immigration
> status; ethnic origin (White vs. visible minority); homeownership; student status |
> Main questionnaire*

Age is available in PUMF only as a grouped derived variable (`dhhgage`); single-year
resolution requires restricted RDC access. `dhhdfc12p` (children Ôëą 12 yrs) has no PUMF
equivalent. Student status (`sdcdgstud`) is suppressed in the PUMF and renders all-NA.

| Requested | 2011 | 2014 | In Study | Note |
|-----------|------|------|----------|------|
| Age (grouped; recoded to 15ÔÇô24, 25ÔÇô54, 55ÔÇô75) | `dhhgage` | `dhhgage` | `age_group_3` | Ôťů Ordered factor: "15-24" / "25-54" / "55-75"; raw `dhhgage` also retained as integer code |
| Sex | `dhh_sex` | `dhh_sex` | `sex` | Ôťů Factor: Male / Female |
| Marital status | `dhhgms` | `dhhgms` | `marital_status` | Ôťů Factor: Single / Married / Common-law / Widowed-Divorced-Separated |
| Household size (number of persons) | `dhhghsz` | `dhhghsz` | `household_size` | Ôťů Ordered factor: 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5+ (recoded from `dhhghsz`; groups 5ÔÇô8 collapsed to "5+") |
| Children ÔëĄ 5 yrs in household | `dhhgle5` | `dhhgle5` | `dhhgle5` | Ôťů Numeric (0 = none, 1 = one or more); present and populated in both cycles |
| Children 6ÔÇô11 yrs in household | `dhhg611` | `dhhg611` | `dhhg611` | Ôťů Numeric (0 = none, 1 = one or more); present and populated in both cycles |
| Children Ôëą 12 yrs in household (count) | unknown | unknown | ÔŁî absent | ÔŁî White-list used `dhhdfc12p`; no PUMF equivalent found in either cycle |
| Education level (4 categories) | `edudh04` | `edudh04` | `education` | Ôťů Ordered factor: Less than secondary Ôćĺ Post-secondary graduate. ÔÜá´ŞĆ Cross-cycle label discrepancy for code 3 ("Other post-secondary" in 2010 vs "Some post-secondary" in 2014; see `data-public/metadata/cchs_value_label_diffs.csv`) |
| Immigration status | `sdcfimm` | `sdcfimm` | `immigration_status` | Ôťů Factor: Non-immigrant / Immigrant / Non-permanent resident |
| Ethnic origin (White vs. visible minority) | `sdcgcgt` | `sdcgcgt` | `visible_minority` | Ôťů Alias `sdcdgcb ÔćÉ sdcgcgt` resolves; Factor: White / Visible minority |
| Living arrangements | `dhhglvg` | `dhhglvg` | `living_arrangements` | Ôťů Factor (8 levels): Unattached alone / Unattached with others / Couple no children / Couple with children / Single parent / Child in parent-sibling household / Child in two-parent household / Other. **Note**: The statistical instructions (┬ž2.2) specify "homeownership" as a predisposing variable; however, no house-ownership construct exists in the CCHS PUMF. `living_arrangements` (household composition) is included as the closest available proxy, per agreement with M.-A. Blanchette (2026-05-15 review). |
| Student status | ÔŁî absent | ÔŁî absent | `student_status` (all NA) | ÔŁî `sdcdgstud` not present in PUMF; column all-NA in current dataset |

---

## 5. Facilitating Variables

> *Household income (5 categories); province/territory of residence (11 categories);
> having a family doctor; employment type (self-employed vs. employee); work schedule
> (full-time vs. part-time); occupation category; job stress level; fruit/vegetable
> consumption; alcohol use; smoking status; BMI/obesity category; physical activity level |
> Main questionnaire*

All 12 facilitating variables are populated in the current pipeline output.
The job stress variable used is **GEN_09** (perceived work stress), stored as `work_stress`,
not GEN_07 (life stress). Province is retained as a 13-level factor (includes the
combined Yukon/NWT/Nunavut territory grouping). Employment type has **2 levels** in the
PUMF (Employee / Self-employed); "Unpaid family worker" is not present.

| Requested | 2011 | 2014 | In Study | Note |
|-----------|------|------|----------|------|
| Household income ÔÇö derived 5-category | `incghh` | `incghh` | `income_hh` | Ôťů Alias `incdghh ÔćÉ incghh` resolves; Ordered factor: < $20k Ôćĺ $80k+ |
| Province / territory of residence | `geogprv` | `geogprv` | `province` | Ôťů Alias `geodgprv ÔćÉ geogprv` resolves; 13-level factor (includes NWT/Yukon/Nunavut combined) |
| Having a regular family doctor | `hcu_1aa` | `hcu_1aa` | `has_family_doctor` | Ôťů Factor: Yes / No |
| Employment type (self-employed vs. employee) | `lbsg31` | `lbsg31` | `employment_type` | Ôťů Factor: Employee / Self-employed (2 levels in PUMF; "Unpaid family worker" suppressed) |
| Work schedule (full-time vs. part-time) | `lbsdpft` | `lbsdpft` | `work_schedule` | Ôťů Alias `lbfdgft ÔćÉ lbsdpft` resolves; Factor: Full-time / Part-time |
| Occupation category (5-group SOC) | `lbsgsoc` | `lbsgsoc` | `occupation_category` | Ôťů Factor: Mgmt/Educ/Arts / Business/Finance / Sales/Services / Trades/Transport / Primary/Processing (2010 descriptive labels applied to both cycles; codes identical) |
| Job / work stress level | `gen_09` | `gen_09` | `work_stress` | Ôťů GEN_09 = perceived **work** stress; ordered factor: Not at all / Not very / A bit / Quite a bit / Extremely stressful |
| Total daily fruit & vegetable consumption | `fvcgtot` | `fvcgtot` | `fruit_veg_daily` | Ôťů Alias `fvcdgtot ÔćÉ fvcgtot` resolves; 3-category derived ordinal (< 5 / 5ÔÇô10 / > 10 servings per day) |
| Alcohol use (type of drinker ÔÇö derived) | `alcdttm` | `alcdttm` | `alcohol_type` | Ôťů Factor (3 levels): Former or never drinker / Occasional drinker / Regular drinker |
| Smoking status (derived) | `smkdsty` | `smkdsty` | `smoking_status` | Ôťů Ordered factor: Never / Former / Occasional / Daily |
| BMI category (derived) | `hwtgisw` | `hwtgisw` | `bmi_category` | Ôťů Ordered factor: Underweight / Normal weight / Overweight / Obese |
| Physical activity index (derived) | `pacdpai` | `pacdpai` | `physical_activity` | Ôťů Ordered factor: Active / Moderately active / Inactive |

---

## 6. Needs Variables

> *Functional limitations (activity limitations); injury status;
> self-perceived general health; self-perceived mental health;
> self-perceived health compared to prior year | Main questionnaire*

Six needs variables are present. The original single-item activity limitation (`rac_1`)
has been replaced by six granular ADL items with semantic names (`adl_meals`,
`adl_errands`, `adl_housework`, `adl_personal_care`, `adl_moving_indoors`,
`adl_finances`), each recoded to a Yes / No factor.
Self-perceived mental health is captured by `gen_02b`.

| Requested | 2011 | 2014 | In Study | Note |
|-----------|------|------|----------|------|
| Functional / activity limitations ÔÇö meal preparation | `adl_01` | `adl_01` | `adl_meals` | Ôťů Factor: Yes / No; semantic rename from `adl_01` |
| Functional / activity limitations ÔÇö appointments / errands | `adl_02` | `adl_02` | `adl_errands` | Ôťů Factor: Yes / No; semantic rename from `adl_02` |
| Functional / activity limitations ÔÇö housework | `adl_03` | `adl_03` | `adl_housework` | Ôťů Factor: Yes / No; semantic rename from `adl_03` |
| Functional / activity limitations ÔÇö personal care | `adl_04` | `adl_04` | `adl_personal_care` | Ôťů Factor: Yes / No; semantic rename from `adl_04` |
| Functional / activity limitations ÔÇö moving inside home | `adl_05` | `adl_05` | `adl_moving_indoors` | Ôťů Factor: Yes / No; semantic rename from `adl_05` |
| Functional / activity limitations ÔÇö personal finances | `adl_06` | `adl_06` | `adl_finances` | Ôťů Factor: Yes / No; semantic rename from `adl_06` |
| Injury status (past 12 months) | `inj_01` | `inj_01` | `injured_past_12m` | Ôťů Factor: Yes / No |
| Self-perceived general health | `gen_01` | `gen_01` | `health_perceived` | Ôťů Ordered factor: Excellent Ôćĺ Poor |
| Self-perceived mental health | `gen_02b` | `gen_02b` | `mental_health_perceived` | Ôťů White-list entry `gen_02a` resolved by alias map to `gen_02b`; ordered factor: Excellent Ôćĺ Poor |
| Self-perceived health compared to 1 year ago | `gen_02` | `gen_02` | `health_vs_prior_year` | Ôťů Ordered factor: Much better Ôćĺ Much worse; GEN_02 = health vs. prior year (not mental health) |

---

## 7. Survey Design Variables

These variables are retained in the analytic dataset to support complex sample survey
analysis and reproducibility checks.

| Variable | Source | In Study | Note |
|----------|--------|----------|------|
| Per-cycle sampling weight | `wtsa_m` / `wts_m` | `wts_m` | Ôťů Full-sample weight for single-cycle analysis |
| Pooled survey weight | derived | `wts_m_pooled` | Ôťů Ellis-computed: `wts_m / 2` following Statistics Canada pooling guidelines |
| Survey cycle | derived | `cchs_cycle_f` | Ôťů Factor: "CCHS 2010" / "CCHS 2014" |
| Complete CCC flag | derived | `flag_complete_ccc` | Ôťů Logical: TRUE if all 17 `cc_*` variables are non-missing |
| Complete predictors flag | derived | `flag_complete_predictors` | Ôťů Logical: TRUE if all predictor variables are non-missing |
| Analytic complete flag | derived | `flag_analytic_complete` | Ôťů Logical: TRUE if complete CCC and complete predictors |

---

## Coverage Summary

| Category | ┬ž2.2 Requested | Available in PUMF | In Study | Absent / all-NA |
|----------|---------------|------------------|----------|-----------------|
| Outcomes (primary + sensitivity) | 2 | 2 | 2 | 0 |
| LOP components | 8 | 8 | 8 | 0 |
| Chronic conditions | 19 | 17 | 17 | 2 (RDC-only) |
| Predisposing | 12 | 11 | 11 | 1 (`dhhdfc12p`) + 1 all-NA (`student_status`) |
| Facilitating | 12 | 12 | 12 | 0 |
| Needs | 10 | 10 | 10 | 0 |
| Survey design | 6 | 6 | 6 | 0 |

**Total:** 69 columns in `cchs_analytic`. Two chronic condition variables (`ccc_300`,
`ccc_185`) and one predisposing variable (`dhhdfc12p`) require RDC access. Student status
(`student_status`) is all-NA due to PUMF suppression of `sdcdgstud`.
